name: wilson-hexa-first-warn
description: PreToolUse guard on Write when file_path ends in `.py` or `.sh` — acts on the hexa-first principle, asking whether a hexa-native equivalent (stdlib, atlas-bound theorem, or `hexa` CLI subcommand) covers the same job before a hand-rolled script lands. Two modes, selected by SIDECAR_HEXA_FIRST_WARN_MODE (WILSON_ accepted): `block` (DEFAULT) — the Write is DENIED at PreToolUse via permissionDecision, emitting an AI-native single-line directive (HEXA_FIRST_BLOCK { trigger, target, ext, action }); the model must pick a hexa-native path or downgrade. `warn` — a single-line advisory (HEXA_FIRST_WARN { ... }) is injected via additionalContext and the Write proceeds unchanged. Any value other than `warn` resolves to `block` — block is the floor. New-file moment only (`Write` matcher, NOT `Edit`) — editing an existing .py file does not nag every time. Whitelist is deliberately narrow (`.py`, `.sh`) — `.js`/`.ts`/`Makefile` etc. are out of scope to avoid noise. Implements hexa-first as enforcement complementing wilson-prefs's principle-text injection — text says "prefer hexa", this stops at the file-creation moment. Opt out entirely: SIDECAR_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1 (or WILSON_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1).
